---
title: "SfM Photogrammetry for Marine Science"
description: "An overview of Structure-from-Motion photogrammetry methods applied to coral reef and benthic habitat surveys."
date: "2025-01-01"
categories: [marine science, photogrammetry, 3D, GIS]
image: ../images/sfm-marine.jpg
---

## Overview

Structure-from-Motion (SfM) photogrammetry enables the creation of precise 3D 
models from overlapping photographs. This page provides an overview of how SfM 
methods can be applied to underwater survey data to map and monitor coral reefs 
and benthic habitats.

## Why SfM for Marine Science?

Traditional survey methods struggle to capture the complex 3D structure of coral 
reefs. SfM offers:

- High-resolution 3D reconstructions from standard cameras
- Quantitative measurements of structural complexity
- Repeatable surveys for change detection over time
- Low-cost relative to LiDAR or multibeam sonar

## Related Projects

- [Reef SfM Workflow](reef-sfm-workflow.qmd) — detailed processing pipeline
- [Benthic Habitat Map](benthic-habitat-map.qmd) — GIS mapping outputs
